introduction: Cessation of breath for more than 15 seconds or any period with bradycardia and/or cyanosis. There may also be hypotonia or pallor in severe cases. Particularly common among very preterm infants with estimated gestational age of < 32 weeks. It may also occur among sick term babies. Prematurity and immaturity of the central respiratory centre causes a separate entity known as “Apnea of Prematurity”.

features: Cessation of breath for more than 15 seconds
Particularly common among very preterm infants with estimated gestational age of < 32 weeks

non_drug: Tactile stimulation such as flicking the sole of the feet
Apnea monitor or pulse oximeter with alarm system for hypoxaemia
Nursing in the prone position with cessation of oral feeds if gastro-oesophageal reflux is suspected
drug: 10% Dextrose-in-Water (4 mL/kg) for the correction of blood glucose
Caffeine citrate in a loading dose of 20 mg/kg oral or slow intravenous and maintenance of 5-10mg/kg per 24 hours

Aminophylline in a loading dose of 6mg/kg slow intravenous and maintenance of 2.5mg/kg 12-hourly until post-conceptional age of 33-34 weeks or until no longer requires oxygen therapy or has been free of apnea for at least 5 days
supportive_measures: Resuscitation: airway clearance, assisted breathing and circulation according to standard protocols

prevention: Early commencement of CPAP in a spontaneously breathing neonate
